title: Writing - Part 1: Work-Life Balance
description: Your class has been discussing the importance of maintaining a healthy work-life balance in modern society.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the factors in your notes. You should explain which factor you believe has the greatest impact on work-life balance, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Employees who work excessively long hours suffer from burnout and health problems.
Smartphones and email mean people are never truly off duty, even at weekends.
The option to work flexibly allows people to manage their personal and professional lives more effectively.

structure.intro: Introduce the concept of work-life balance and state the two factors you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first factor and how it affects employees' quality of life.
structure.body2: Discuss the second factor and compare its influence to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which factor you regard as most significant and explain why.

content.modelAnswer:
A healthy work-life balance is essential for both individual wellbeing and productivity. Yet for many people in today's fast-paced world, achieving this balance is increasingly difficult. Two factors that have a significant impact are long working hours and the constant connectivity enabled by technology.

Working excessively long hours has well-documented negative effects on health and wellbeing. Employees who consistently work beyond their contracted hours are more likely to experience stress, burnout and even physical illness. Moreover, insufficient time for rest and personal activities damages relationships and reduces job satisfaction over time.

Technology has further blurred the boundaries between work and personal life. With smartphones and email, many employees feel pressure to respond to work messages outside of office hours, making it almost impossible to fully switch off. This constant connectivity can be just as damaging as long working hours, as the psychological break from work never fully occurs.

In my view, technology's role is the more harmful factor. Even if official working hours are reasonable, the expectation of constant availability effectively extends the working day indefinitely. Employers and employees must agree on clear boundaries around out-of-hours communication to genuinely protect personal time.
